`total.token.count` * **Type:** ==counter== (token)

task_description: Executes a chat turn from a message list (max one system message; last must be USER) using the configured provider. Optional tools may be invoked by the model; token metrics are reported. JSON-schema formats in `configuration` require provider support.

sources.title: Content sources used during RAG retrieval

thinking.title: Model's Thinking Output
thinking.description:
Contains the model's internal reasoning or 'thinking' text, if the model supports it and 'returnThinking' is enabled.
This may include intermediate reasoning steps, such as chain-of-thought explanations. Null if thinking is not supported, not enabled, or not returned by the model.

aiResponse.title: AI Response
aiResponse.description: The generated response from the AI (Deprecated: use `completion` instead)

jsonOutput.title: LLM output for `JSON` response format
jsonOutput.description: The result of the LLM completion for response format of type `JSON`, null otherwise.

textOutput.title: LLM output for `TEXT` response format
textOutput.description: The result of the LLM completion for response format of type `TEXT` (default), null otherwise.

outputFiles.title: URIs of the generated files in Kestra's internal storage

properties.title: Run chat completion with tools

$metrics.name: input.token.count
$metrics.description: Large Language Model (LLM) input token count

$metrics.name: output.token.count
$metrics.description: Large Language Model (LLM) output token count

$metrics.name: total.token.count
$metrics.description: Large Language Model (LLM) total token count

$examples.code:
id: chat_completion
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING

tasks:
  - id: chat_completion
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_API_KEY') }}"
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
    messages:
      - type: SYSTEM
        content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity.
      - type: USER
        content: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
$examples.title: Chat completion with Google Gemini

$examples.code:
id: chat_completion_with_tools
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING

tasks:
  - id: chat_completion_with_tools
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_API_KEY') }}"
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
    messages:
      - type: SYSTEM
        content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity.
      - type: USER
        content: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
    tools:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.GoogleCustomWebSearch
        apiKey: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_SEARCH_API_KEY') }}"
        csi: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_SEARCH_CSI') }}"
$examples.title: Chat Completion with Google Gemini and a WebSearch tool

$examples.code:
id: structured-output
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING
    defaults: |
      Hello, my name is John. I was born on January 1, 2000.

tasks:
  - id: ai-agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    configuration:
      responseFormat:
        type: JSON
        jsonSchema:
          type: object
          properties:
            name:
              type: string
            birth:
              type: string
      messages:
      - type: USER
        content: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
$examples.title:
Extract structured outputs with a JSON schema.
Not all model providers support JSON schema; in those cases, you have to specify the schema in the prompt.

tools.title: Tools that the LLM may use to augment its response

messages.title: Chat Messages
messages.description: The list of chat messages for the current conversation. There can be only one system message, and the last message must be a user message

properties.description: Executes a chat turn from a message list (max one system message; last must be USER) using the configured provider. Optional tools may be invoked by the model; token metrics are reported. JSON-schema formats in `configuration` require provider support.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.AIAgent.title: Expose a nested AI Agent as a tool

$examples.code:
id: ai-agent-with-agent-tools
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING
    defaults: |
      Each flow can produce outputs that can be consumed by other flows. This is a list property, so that your flow can produce as many outputs as you need.
      Each output needs to have an ID (the name of the output), a type (the same types you know from inputs, e.g., STRING, URI, or JSON), and a value, which is the actual output value that will be stored in internal storage and passed to other flows when needed.
tasks:
  - id: ai-agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    systemMessage: Summarize the user message, then translate it into French using the provided tool.
    prompt: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
    tools:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.AIAgent
        description: Translation expert
        systemMessage: You are an expert in translating text between multiple languages
        provider:
          type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
          modelName: gemini-2.5-flash-lite
          apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
$examples.title: Call an AI agent as a tool

name.title: Agent name
name.description: It must be set to a different value than the default in case you want to have multiple agents used as tools in the same task.

tools.title: Tools that the LLM may use to augment its response

description.title: Agent description
description.description: The description will be used to instruct the LLM what the tool is doing.

systemMessage.title: System message
systemMessage.description: The system message for the language model

contentRetrievers.title: Content retrievers
contentRetrievers.description: Some content retrievers, like WebSearch, can also be used as tools. However, when configured as content retrievers, they will always be used, whereas tools are only invoked when the LLM decides to use them.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.AIAgent.description: Wraps another AI Agent so the parent agent can invoke it as a tool. Provide a unique `name` and `description` per tool; the name defaults to `tool`. Content retrievers configured here always run, while other tools are invoked only when the LLM selects them.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.A2AClient.title: Invoke remote AI agent over A2A

$examples.code:
id: ai-agent-with-agent-tools
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING
    defaults: |
      Each flow can produce outputs that can be consumed by other flows. This is a list property, so that your flow can produce as many outputs as you need.
      Each output needs to have an ID (the name of the output), a type (the same types you know from inputs, e.g., STRING, URI, or JSON), and a value, which is the actual output value that will be stored in internal storage and passed to other flows when needed.
tasks:
  - id: ai-agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"

name.title: Agent name
name.description: It must be set to a different value than the default in case you want to have multiple agents used as tools in the same task.

serverUrl.title: Server URL
serverUrl.description: The URL of the remote agent A2A server

description.title: Agent description
description.description: The description will be used to instruct the LLM what the tool is doing.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.A2AClient.description: Forwards prompts to a remote AI Agent using the A2A protocol and returns its response. Provide a meaningful `name` and `description` so the parent agent can choose the tool; the name defaults to `tool`. Requires `serverUrl` to reach the remote agent.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow.title: Execute Kestra flows from an agent

$examples.code:
id: agent_calling_flows_explicitly
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: use_case
    type: SELECT
    description: Your Orchestration Use Case
    defaults: Hello World
    values:
      - Business Automation
      - Business Processes
      - Data Engineering Pipeline
      - Data Warehouse and Analytics
      - Infrastructure Automation
      - Microservices and APIs
      - Hello World

tasks:
  - id: agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    prompt: Execute a flow that best matches the {{ inputs.use_case }} use case selected by the user
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    tools:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
        namespace: tutorial
        flowId: business-automation
        description: Business Automation

      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
        namespace: tutorial
        flowId: business-processes
        description: Business Processes

      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
        namespace: tutorial
        flowId: data-engineering-pipeline
        description: Data Engineering Pipeline

      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
        namespace: tutorial
        flowId: dwh-and-analytics
        description: Data Warehouse and Analytics

      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
        namespace: tutorial
        flowId: file-processing
        description: File Processing

      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
        namespace: tutorial
        flowId: hello-world
        description: Hello World

      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
        namespace: tutorial
        flowId: infrastructure-automation
        description: Infrastructure Automation

      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
        namespace: tutorial
        flowId: microservices-and-apis
        description: Microservices and APIs
$examples.title: Call a Kestra flow as a tool, explicitly defining the flow ID and namespace in the tool definition

$examples.code:
id: agent_calling_flows_implicitly
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: use_case
    type: SELECT
    description: Your Orchestration Use Case
    defaults: Hello World
    values:
      - Business Automation
      - Business Processes
      - Data Engineering Pipeline
      - Data Warehouse and Analytics
      - Infrastructure Automation
      - Microservices and APIs
      - Hello World

tasks:
  - id: agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    prompt: |
      Execute a flow that best matches the {{ inputs.use_case }} use case selected by the user. Use the following mapping of use cases to flow IDs:
      - Business Automation: business-automation
      - Business Processes: business-processes
      - Data Engineering Pipeline: data-engineering-pipeline
      - Data Warehouse and Analytics: dwh-and-analytics
      - Infrastructure Automation: infrastructure-automation
      - Microservices and APIs: microservices-and-apis
      - Hello World: hello-world
      Remember that all those flows are in the tutorial namespace.
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    tools:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow
$examples.title: Call a Kestra flow as a tool, implicitly passing the flow ID and namespace in the prompt

flowId.title: Flow ID of the flow that should be called

inputs.title: Input values that should be passed to flow's execution
inputs.description: Any inputs passed by the LLM will override those defined here.

labels.title: Labels that should be added to the flow's execution
labels.description: Any labels passed by the LLM will override those defined here.

anyOf.title: Revision of the flow that should be called
Revision of the flow that should be called

revision.title: Revision of the flow that should be called

namespace.title: Namespace of the flow that should be called

description.title: Description of the flow if not already provided inside the flow itself
description.description:
Use it only if you define the flow in the tool definition.
The LLM needs a tool description to identify whether to call it.
If the flow has a description, the tool will use it. Otherwise, the description property must be explicitly defined.

anyOf.title: Schedule the flow execution at a later date
anyOf.description: If the LLM sets a scheduleDate, it will override the one defined here.

anyOf.title: Schedule the flow execution at a later date
anyOf.description: If the LLM sets a scheduleDate, it will override the one defined here.

scheduleDate.title: Schedule the flow execution at a later date
scheduleDate.description: If the LLM sets a scheduleDate, it will override the one defined here.

anyOf.title: Whether the flow should inherit labels from this execution that triggered it
anyOf.description:
By default, labels are not inherited. If you set this option to `true`, the flow execution will inherit all labels from the agent's execution.
Any labels passed by the LLM will override those defined here.

anyOf.title: Whether the flow should inherit labels from this execution that triggered it
anyOf.description:
By default, labels are not inherited. If you set this option to `true`, the flow execution will inherit all labels from the agent's execution.
Any labels passed by the LLM will override those defined here.

inheritLabels.title: Whether the flow should inherit labels from this execution that triggered it
inheritLabels.description:
By default, labels are not inherited. If you set this option to `true`, the flow execution will inherit all labels from the agent's execution.
Any labels passed by the LLM will override those defined here.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraFlow.description: Triggers Kestra flows as tools, either predefined (`kestra_flow_<namespace>_<flowId>`) or generic (`kestra_flow` with namespace/flowId provided by the prompt). A description is mandatory from the flow or the tool `description`; inputs, labels, and schedule provided by the LLM override tool defaults. Labels are not inherited unless `inheritLabels=true`, while the correlationId is inherited when none is supplied.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraTask.title: Expose Kestra runnable tasks as tools

$examples.code:
id: call_a_kestra_task
    namespace: company.ai

    tasks:
      - id: agent
        type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
        provider:
          type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
          modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
          apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
        tools:
          - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraTask
            tasks:
              - id: log
                type: io.kestra.plugin.core.log.Log
                message: "..." # This is a placeholder; the agent will fill it.
        prompt: "Log the following message: 'Hello World!'"
$examples.title: Call a Kestra runnable task as a tool, letting the agent set the `message` property for you

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.KestraTask.description: Creates one tool per runnable task named `kestra_task_<taskId>`. Properties you set stay fixed; set a required property to `...` to force the agent to supply it, and unset optionals may be filled by the agent. `anyOf` schemas are flattened to a single branch because many models do not support `anyOf`; the generated schema appears in debug logs.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Milvus.title: Store embeddings in Milvus

$examples.code:
id: document_ingestion
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: ingest
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    embeddings:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Milvus
      # Use either `uri` or `host`/`port`:
      # For gRPC (typical): milvus://localhost:19530
      # For HTTP: http://localhost:9091
      uri: "http://localhost:19200"
      token: "{{ kv('MILVUS_TOKEN') }}"  # omit if auth is disabled
      collectionName: embeddings
    fromExternalURLs:
      - https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kestra-io/docs/refs/heads/main/content/blogs/release-0-24.md
$examples.title: Ingest documents into a Milvus embedding store

uri.title: URI
uri.description:
Connection URI. Use either `uri` OR `host`/`port` (not both).
Examples:
- gRPC (typical): "milvus://host:19530"
- HTTP: "http://host:9091"

host.title: Host
host.description: Milvus host name (used when `uri` is not set). Default: "localhost".

anyOf.title: Port
anyOf.description: Milvus port (used when `uri` is not set). Typical: 19530 (gRPC) or 9091 (HTTP). Default: 19530.

anyOf.title: Port
anyOf.description: Milvus port (used when `uri` is not set). Typical: 19530 (gRPC) or 9091 (HTTP). Default: 19530.

port.title: Port
port.description: Milvus port (used when `uri` is not set). Typical: 19530 (gRPC) or 9091 (HTTP). Default: 19530.

token.title: Token
token.description: Milvus auth token. Required if authentication is enabled; omit for local deployments without auth.

password.title: Password
password.description: Required when authentication/TLS is enabled. See https://milvus.io/docs/authenticate.md

username.title: Username
username.description: Required when authentication/TLS is enabled. See https://milvus.io/docs/authenticate.md

indexType.title: Index type
indexType.description: Vector index type (e.g., IVF_FLAT, IVF_SQ8, HNSW). Depends on Milvus deployment and dataset.

metricType.title: Metric type
metricType.description: Similarity metric (e.g., L2, IP, COSINE). Should match the embedding provider’s expected metric.

idFieldName.title: ID field name
idFieldName.description: Field name for document IDs. Default depends on collection schema.

databaseName.title: Database name
databaseName.description: Logical database to use. If not provided, the default database is used.

textFieldName.title: Text field name
textFieldName.description: Field name for original text. Default depends on collection schema.

collectionName.title: Collection name
collectionName.description: Target collection. Created automatically if it does not exist. Default: "default".

vectorFieldName.title: Vector field name
vectorFieldName.description: Field name for the embedding vector. Must match the index definition and embedding dimensionality.

consistencyLevel.title: Consistency level
consistencyLevel.description: Read/write consistency level. Common values include STRONG, BOUNDED, or EVENTUALLY (depends on client/version).

anyOf.title: Auto flush on delete
anyOf.description: If true, flush after delete operations.

anyOf.title: Auto flush on delete
anyOf.description: If true, flush after delete operations.

autoFlushOnDelete.title: Auto flush on delete
autoFlushOnDelete.description: If true, flush after delete operations.

anyOf.title: Auto flush on insert
anyOf.description: If true, flush after insert operations. Setting it to false can improve throughput.

anyOf.title: Auto flush on insert
anyOf.description: If true, flush after insert operations. Setting it to false can improve throughput.

autoFlushOnInsert.title: Auto flush on insert
autoFlushOnInsert.description: If true, flush after insert operations. Setting it to false can improve throughput.

metadataFieldName.title: Metadata field name
metadataFieldName.description: Field name for metadata. Default depends on collection schema.

anyOf.title: Retrieve embeddings on search
anyOf.description: If true, return stored embeddings along with matches. Default: false.

anyOf.title: Retrieve embeddings on search
anyOf.description: If true, return stored embeddings along with matches. Default: false.

retrieveEmbeddingsOnSearch.title: Retrieve embeddings on search
retrieveEmbeddingsOnSearch.description: If true, return stored embeddings along with matches. Default: false.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Milvus.description: Connects via URI or host/port with token-based auth; creates the target collection if missing. Metric/index/consistency options map to Milvus settings; `drop=true` clears the collection. Use defaults for host=localhost, port=19530, secure gRPC unless overridden.

$examples.code:
id: mcp_client_sse
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING
    defaults: Find 2 restaurants in Lille, France with the best reviews

  - id: agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    prompt: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini

sseUrl.title: SSE URL of the MCP server

headers.title: Custom headers

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.SseMcpClient.description: Connects to an MCP server that streams Server-Sent Events and exposes its tools to the agent. Requires `sseUrl`; `timeout` is optional. Request/response logging is disabled by default; add `headers` for auth tokens.

indexes.title: Metadata Index Definitions
indexes.description:
List of SQL index definitions for metadata columns (e.g., 'INDEX idx_text (text)').
 Used only with COLUMN_PER_KEY storage mode.

fieldName.title: Name of the column used as the unique ID in the database

tableName.title: Name of the table where embeddings will be stored

anyOf.title: Whether to create the table if it doesn't exist
Whether to create the table if it doesn't exist

createTable.title: Whether to create the table if it doesn't exist

databaseUrl.title: Database URL of the MariaDB database (e.g., jdbc:mariadb://host:port/dbname)

columnDefinitions.title: Metadata Column Definitions
columnDefinitions.description:
List of SQL column definitions for metadata fields (e.g., 'text TEXT', 'source TEXT').
  Required only when using COLUMN_PER_KEY storage mode.

metadataStorageMode.title: Metadata Storage Mode
metadataStorageMode.description:
Determines how metadata is stored:
    - COLUMN_PER_KEY: Use individual columns for each metadata field (requires columnDefinitions and indexes).
    - COMBINED_JSON (default): Store metadata as a JSON object in a single column.
  If columnDefinitions and indexes are provided, COLUMN_PER_KEY must be used.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.MariaDB.description: Persists embeddings to a MariaDB table; create/drop behavior is controlled by `createTable` and `drop`. Metadata defaults to COMBINED_JSON unless COLUMN_PER_KEY is configured with column/index definitions. Requires valid JDBC URL and credentials.

$examples.title: Ingest documents into a PGVector embedding store

table.title: The table to store embeddings in

anyOf.title: Whether to use use an IVFFlat index
anyOf.description: An IVFFlat index divides vectors into lists, and then searches a subset of those lists closest to the query vector. It has faster build times and uses less memory than HNSW but has lower query performance (in terms of speed-recall tradeoff).

anyOf.title: Whether to use use an IVFFlat index
anyOf.description: An IVFFlat index divides vectors into lists, and then searches a subset of those lists closest to the query vector. It has faster build times and uses less memory than HNSW but has lower query performance (in terms of speed-recall tradeoff).

useIndex.title: Whether to use use an IVFFlat index
useIndex.description: An IVFFlat index divides vectors into lists, and then searches a subset of those lists closest to the query vector. It has faster build times and uses less memory than HNSW but has lower query performance (in terms of speed-recall tradeoff).

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.PGVector.description: Uses the PostgreSQL pgvector extension to persist embeddings in the given table. `drop=true` recreates the table; optional IVF index (`useIndex`) defaults to false. Ensure pgvector extension is installed and the user can create tables/indexes.

$examples.code:
id: docker_mcp_client
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING
    defaults: Create a file 'hello.txt' with the content "Hello World" in the /tmp directory.

tasks:
  - id: agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
    prompt: "{{ inputs.prompt }}"
    tools:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.DockerMcpClient
        image: mcp/filesystem
        command: ["/tmp"]
        # Mount the container path to the task working directory to access the generated file
        binds: ["{{workingDir}}:/tmp"]
    outputFiles:
      - hello.txt
$examples.title: Agent calling an MCP server in a Docker container and generating output files

command.title: MCP client command, as a list of command parts

anyOf.title: Whether Docker should verify TLS certificates
Whether Docker should verify TLS certificates

dockerTlsVerify.title: Whether Docker should verify TLS certificates

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.DockerMcpClient.description: Launches an MCP server inside a Docker container and exposes its tools to the agent. Requires an `image`; optional `command`, `env`, and `binds` control the container. Docker host defaults to the detected runtime; `logEvents` defaults to false. Provide registry credentials and TLS settings when pulling from private registries.

responseFormat.title: Response format
responseFormat.description:
Defines the expected output format. Default is plain text.
Some providers allow requesting JSON or schema-constrained outputs, but support varies and may be incompatible with tool use.
When using a JSON schema, the output will be returned under the key `jsonOutput`.

anyOf.title: Return Thinking
anyOf.description:
Controls whether to return the model's internal reasoning or 'thinking' text, if available. When enabled,
the reasoning content is extracted from the response and made available in the AiMessage object.
It Does not trigger the thinking process itself—only affects whether the output is parsed and returned.

returnThinking.title: Return Thinking
returnThinking.description:
Controls whether to return the model's internal reasoning or 'thinking' text, if available. When enabled,
the reasoning content is extracted from the response and made available in the AiMessage object.
It Does not trigger the thinking process itself—only affects whether the output is parsed and returned.

anyOf.title: Enable Thinking
anyOf.description:
Enables internal reasoning ('thinking') in supported language models, allowing the model to perform intermediate reasoning steps
before producing a final output; this is useful for complex tasks like multi-step problem solving or decision making, but may
increase token usage and response time, and is only applicable to compatible models.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Elasticse.title: Store embeddings in Elasticsearch

$examples.code:
id: document_ingestion
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: ingest
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    embeddings:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Elasticsearch
      connection:
        hosts:
          - http://localhost:9200
    fromExternalURLs:
      - https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kestra-io/docs/refs/heads/main/content/blogs/release-0-24.md
$examples.title: Ingest documents into an Elasticsearch embedding store (requires Elasticsearch 8.15+)

indexName.title: The name of the index to store embeddings

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Elasticse.description: Targets an Elasticsearch 8.15+ cluster using the provided hosts/index; when `drop=true` the index is deleted. Supports basic auth, custom headers, path prefix, and trust-all TLS for self-signed certs.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVS.title: Prototype embeddings in Kestra KV

$examples.code:
id: document_ingestion
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: ingest
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    embeddings:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVStore

$examples.title: Ingest documents into a KV embedding store.\nWARNING: the `KestraKVStore` embeddings are for quick prototyping only; since they are stored in a KV store and loaded into memory, this won't scale with large numbers of documents.

kvName.title: The name of the KV pair to use

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVS.description: Stores embeddings in-memory and persists them to Kestra namespace KV on completion. Suitable for small demos; not scalable. `drop=true` discards any previously saved KV snapshot.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.TavilyWebS.title: Retrieve web results with Tavily

Maximum number of results to return

maxResults.title: Maximum number of results to return

io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.TavilyWebS.description: Uses Tavily Search to fetch live web context for RAG. Requires API key; `maxResults` caps returned snippets and defaults to 3. Requests count against Tavily quotas.

completion.title: Generated text completion

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.GoogleCustomWeb.title: Search the web with Google CSE

$examples.code:
id: agent_searching_web
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING
    defaults: What is the latest Kestra release and what new features does it include?

tasks:
  - id: agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
    prompt: "{{ inputs.prompt }}"
    tools:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.GoogleCustomWebSearch
        apiKey: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_SEARCH_API_KEY') }}"
        csi: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_SEARCH_CSI') }}"
$examples.title: Agent using Google Custom Search for web queries

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.GoogleCustomWeb.description: Runs queries through Google Custom Search and returns results to the agent. Requires a Google API key and Custom Search Engine ID (`csi`/`cx`); usage is subject to your CSE quotas and filters.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.StreamableHttpM.title: Call MCP server over HTTP streaming

$examples.code:
id: mcp_client_sse
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING
    defaults: Find the 2 restaurants in Lille, France with the best reviews.

tasks:
  - id: agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    prompt: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    tools:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.StreamableHttpMcpClient
        url: https://mcp.apify.com/?actors=compass/crawler-google-places
        timeout: PT5M
        headers:
          Authorization: Bearer {{ kv('APIFY_API_TOKEN') }}
$examples.title: Agent calling an MCP server via SSE

headers.title: Custom headers
headers.description: Useful, for example, for adding authentication tokens via the `Authorization` header.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.StreamableHttpM.description: Connects to an MCP server via HTTP streaming (chunked responses) and surfaces its tools to the agent. Requires `url`; `timeout`, `headers`, `logRequests`, and `logResponses` are optional and default to provider values with logging off.

content.title: Extracted text segment
content.description: A snippet of text relevant to the user's query, typically a sentence, paragraph, or other discrete unit of text.

metadata.title: Source metadata
metadata.description: Key-value pairs providing context about the origin of the content, such as URLs, document titles, or other relevant attributes.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.SqlDatabas.title: Retrieve context from SQL (experimental)

$examples.code:
id: rag
namespace: company.ai

tasks:

    chatProvider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini

      apiKey: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_API_KEY') }}"

io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.EmbeddingS.title: Retrieve context from an embedding store

$examples.code:
id: agent_with_rag
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: ingest
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
      googleApiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    embeddings:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVStore
    drop: true
    fromDocuments:
      - content: Paris is the capital of France with a population of over 2.1 million people
      - content: The Eiffel Tower is the most famous landmark in Paris at 330 meters tall

  - id: agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-2.0-flash
      googleApiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    contentRetrievers:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.EmbeddingStoreRetriever
        embeddings:
          type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVStore
        embeddingProvider:
          type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
          modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
          googleApiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
        maxResults: 3
        minScore: 0.0
    prompt: What is the capital of France and how many people live there?
$examples.title: Use RAG with AIAgent using an embedding store content retriever. This example ingests documents into a KV embedding store and then uses an AI agent with the EmbeddingStoreRetriever to answer questions grounded in the ingested data.

$examples.code:
id: multi_store_rag
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: agent
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.agent.AIAgent
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-2.0-flash
      googleApiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    contentRetrievers:
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.EmbeddingStoreRetriever
        embeddings:
          type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Pinecone
          pineconeApiKey: "{{ kv('PINECONE_API_KEY') }}"
          index: technical-docs
        embeddingProvider:
          type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.OpenAI
          googleApiKey: "{{ kv('OPENAI_API_KEY') }}"
          modelName: text-embedding-3-small
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.EmbeddingStoreRetriever
        embeddings:
          type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Qdrant
          host: localhost
          port: 6333
          collectionName: business-docs
        embeddingProvider:
          type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
          modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
          googleApiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
      - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.TavilyWebSearch
        tavilyApiKey: "{{ kv('TAVILY_API_KEY') }}"
    prompt: What are the latest trends in data orchestration?

io.kestra.plugin.ai.retriever.EmbeddingS.description: Builds a content retriever over the configured embedding store using a query embedding from `embeddingProvider`. Results are filtered by `maxResults` and `minScore` (0–1). The store is not mutated; ensure the embedding model dimension matches stored vectors.

type.title: Response format type
type.description:
Specifies how the LLM should return output.
Allowed values:
- TEXT (default): free-form natural language.
- JSON: structured output validated against a JSON Schema.

jsonSchema.title: JSON Schema (used when type = JSON)
jsonSchema.description:
Provide a JSON Schema describing the expected structure of the response.
In Kestra flows, define the schema in YAML (it is still a JSON Schema object).
Example (YAML):
```yaml
  responseFormat:
    type: JSON
    jsonSchema:
      type: object
      required: ["category", "priority"]
      properties:
        category:
          type: string
          enum: ["ACCOUNT", "BILLING", "TECHNICAL", "GENERAL"]
        priority:
          type: string
          enum: ["LOW", "MEDIUM", "HIGH"]
```
Note: Provider support for strict schema enforcement varies. If unsupported,
guide the model about the expected output structure via the prompt and validate downstream.

jsonSchemaDescription.title: Schema description (optional)
jsonSchemaDescription.description:
Natural-language description of the schema to help the model produce the right fields.
Example: "Classify a customer ticket into category and priority."

hosts.title: List of HTTP Elasticsearch servers
hosts.description: Must be a URI like `https://example.com:9200` with scheme and port

headers.title: List of HTTP headers to be sent with every request
headers.description: Each item is a `key: value` string, e.g., `Authorization: Token XYZ`

pathPrefix.title: Path prefix for all HTTP requests
pathPrefix.description: If set to `/my/path`, each client request becomes `/my/path/` + endpoint. Useful when Elasticsearch is behind a proxy providing a base path; do not use otherwise.

anyOf.description: Use this if the server uses a self-signed SSL certificate

task_markdown:
--- title: ChatCompletion editLink: false description: "Run chat completion with tools" icon:  ---

ChatCompletion

```yaml type: "io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion" ``` Run chat completion with tools Executes a chat turn from a message list (max one system message; last must be USER) using the configured provider. Optional tools may be invoked by the model; token metrics are reported. JSON-schema formats in `configuration` require provider support. ## Examples > Chat completion with Google Gemini ```yaml id: chat_completion namespace: company.ai inputs: - id: prompt type: STRING tasks: - id: chat_completion type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion provider: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini apiKey: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_API_KEY') }}" modelName: gemini-2.5-flash messages: - type: SYSTEM content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity. - type: USER content: "{{inputs.prompt}}" ``` > Chat Completion with Google Gemini and a WebSearch tool ```yaml id: chat_completion_with_tools namespace: company.ai inputs: - id: prompt type: STRING tasks: - id: chat_completion_with_tools type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion provider: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini apiKey: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_API_KEY') }}" modelName: gemini-2.5-flash messages: - type: SYSTEM content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity. - type: USER content: "{{inputs.prompt}}" tools: - type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.tool.GoogleCustomWebSearch apiKey: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_SEARCH_API_KEY') }}" csi: "{{ kv('GOOGLE_SEARCH_CSI') }}" ``` > Extract structured outputs with a JSON schema. Not all model providers support JSON schema; in those cases, you have to specify the schema in the prompt. ```yaml id: structured-output namespace: company.ai inputs: - id: prompt type: STRING defaults: | Hello, my name is John. I was born on January 1, 2000. tasks: - id: ai-agent type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion provider: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini modelName: gemini-2.5-flash apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}" configuration: responseFormat: type: JSON jsonSchema: type: object properties: name: type: string birth: type: string messages: - type: USER content: "{{inputs.prompt}}" ``` ## Properties ### `messages` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** [==ChatMessage==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.domain.chatmessage) * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Chat Messages** > The list of chat messages for the current conversation. There can be only one system message, and the last message must be a user message ### `provider` * **Type:** * [==AmazonBedrock==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.amazonbedrock) * [==Anthropic==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.anthropic) * [==AzureOpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.azureopenai) * [==DashScope==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.dashscope) * [==DeepSeek==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.deepseek) * [==GitHubModels==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.githubmodels) * [==GoogleGemini==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlegemini) * [==GoogleVertexAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlevertexai) * [==HuggingFace==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.huggingface) * [==LocalAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.localai) * [==MistralAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.mistralai) * [==OciGenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ocigenai) * [==Ollama==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ollama) * [==OpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openai) * [==OpenRouter==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openrouter) * [==WatsonxAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.watsonxai) * [==WorkersAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.workersai) * [==ZhiPuAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.zhipuai) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ✔️ **Language Model Provider** ### `configuration` * **Type:** [==ChatConfiguration==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.domain.chatconfiguration) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `{}` **Chat configuration** ### `tools` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ **Tools that the LLM may use to augment its response** ## In Kestra flows, define the schema in YAML (it is still a JSON Schema object). Example (YAML): ```yaml responseFormat: type: JSON jsonSchema: type: object required: ["category", "priority"] properties: category: type: string enum: ["ACCOUNT", "BILLING", "TECHNICAL", "GENERAL"] priority: type: string enum: ["LOW", "MEDIUM", "HIGH"] ``` Note: Provider support for strict schema enforcement varies. If unsupported, guide the model about the expected output structure via the prompt and validate downstream. ##### `jsonSchemaDescription` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Schema description (optional)** > Natural-language description of the schema to help the model produce the right fields. This limits the length of the output. ##### `responseFormat` * **Type:** [==ChatConfiguration-ResponseFormat==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.domain.chatconfiguration-responseformat) * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ **Response format** > Defines the expected output format. Default is plain text. Some providers allow requesting JSON or schema-constrained outputs, but support varies and may be incompatible with tool use. When using a JSON schema, the output will be returned under the key `jsonOutput`. ##### `returnThinking` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Return Thinking** > Controls whether to return the model's internal reasoning or 'thinking' text, if available. When enabled, the reasoning content is extracted from the response and made available in the AiMessage object. It Does not trigger the thinking process itself—only affects whether the output is parsed and returned. ##### `seed` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Seed** > Optional random seed for reproducibility. Provide a positive integer (e.g., 42, 1234). Using the same seed with identical settings produces repeatable outputs. ##### `temperature` * **Type:** * ==number== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Temperature** > Controls randomness in generation. Typical range is 0.0–1.0. Lower values (e.g., 0.2) make outputs more focused and deterministic, while higher values (e.g., 0.7–1.0) increase creativity and variability. ##### `thinkingBudgetTokens` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Thinking Token Budget** > Specifies the maximum number of tokens allocated as a budget for internal reasoning processes, such as generating intermediate thoughts or chain-of-thought sequences, allowing the model to perform multi-step reasoning before producing the final output. ##### `thinkingEnabled` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Enable Thinking** > Enables internal reasoning ('thinking') in supported language models, allowing the model to perform intermediate reasoning steps before producing a final output; this is useful for complex tasks like multi-step problem solving or decision making, but may increase token usage and response time, and is only applicable to compatible models. ##### `topK` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Top-K** > Limits sampling to the top K most likely tokens at each step. Typical values are between 20 and 100. Smaller values reduce randomness; larger values allow more diverse outputs. ##### `topP` * **Type:** * ==number== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Top-P (nucleus sampling)** > Selects from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability is ≤ topP. Typical values are 0.8–0.95. Lower values make the output more focused, higher values increase diversity. ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.domain.TokenUsage` #### Properties ##### `inputTokenCount` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Dynamic:** **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Model name** ##### `type` * **Type:** ==object== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ✔️ ##### `baseUrl` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `https://router.huggingface.co/v1` **API base URL** ##### `caPem` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **CA PEM certificate content** > CA certificate as text, used to verify SSL/TLS connections when using custom endpoints. ##### `clientPem` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Client PEM certificate content** > PEM client certificate as text, used to authenticate the connection to enterprise AI endpoints. ## Metrics ### `input.token.count` * **Type:** ==counter== (token) ### `output.token.count` * **Type:** ==counter== (token) ### `total.token.count` * **Type:** ==counter== (token)

$examples.code:
id: text_categorization
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: categorize
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.Classification
    prompt: "Categorize the sentiment of: I love this product!"
    classes:
      - positive
      - negative
      - neutral
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash

caPem.description: CA certificate as text, used to verify SSL/TLS connections when using custom endpoints.

baseUrl.title: Base URL
baseUrl.description: Custom base URL to override the default endpoint (useful for local tests, WireMock, or enterprise gateways).

clientPem.title: Client PEM certificate content
clientPem.description: PEM client certificate as text, used to authenticate the connection to enterprise AI endpoints.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemin.description: Supports Gemini chat, embeddings, and images. Tools do not support JSON Schema `anyOf`, and tools cannot be combined with responseFormat; configure either but not both.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.AmazonBedro.title: Use Amazon Bedrock models

$examples.code:
id: chat_completion
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING

tasks:
  - id: chat_completion
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.AmazonBedrock
      accessKeyId: "{{ kv('AWS_ACCESS_KEY') }}"
      secretAccessKey: "{{ kv('AWS_SECRET_KEY') }}"
      modelName: anthropic.claude-3-sonnet-20240229-v1:0
      thinkingBudgetTokens: 1024
    messages:
      - type: SYSTEM
        content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity.
      - type: USER
        content: "{{inputs.prompt}}"

io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.AmazonBedro.description: Invokes Bedrock-hosted chat/embedding models with AWS credentials. Supports standard AWS region/auth settings; ensure model IDs match your Bedrock region and account access.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleVerte.title: Use Google Vertex AI models

$examples.code:
id: chat_completion
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING

tasks:
  - id: chat_completion
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleVertexAI
      endpoint: your-vertex-ai-endpoint
      location: your-google-cloud-region
      project: your-google-cloud-project-id
    messages:
      - type: SYSTEM
        content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity.
      - type: USER
        content: "{{inputs.prompt}}"

anyOf.title: Seed
anyOf.description: Optional random seed for reproducibility. Provide a positive integer (e.g., 42, 1234). Using the same seed with identical settings produces repeatable outputs.

anyOf.title: Top-K
anyOf.description: Limits sampling to the top K most likely tokens at each step. Typical values are between 20 and 100. Smaller values reduce randomness; larger values allow more diverse outputs.

anyOf.title: Top-P (nucleus sampling)
anyOf.description: Selects from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability is ≤ topP. Typical values are 0.8–0.95. Lower values make the output more focused, higher values increase diversity.

responseFormat.title: Response format
responseFormat.description:
Defines the expected output format. Default is plain text.
Some providers allow requesting JSON or schema-constrained outputs, but support varies and may be incompatible with tool use.
When using a JSON schema, the output will be returned under the key `jsonOutput`.

anyOf.title: Return Thinking
anyOf.description:
Controls whether to return the model's internal reasoning or 'thinking' text, if available. When enabled,
the reasoning content is extracted from the response and made available in the AiMessage object.
It Does not trigger the thinking process itself—only affects whether the output is parsed and returned.

anyOf.title: Return Thinking
anyOf.description:
Controls whether to return the model's internal reasoning or 'thinking' text, if available. When enabled,
the reasoning content is extracted from the response and made available in the AiMessage object.
It Does not trigger the thinking process itself—only affects whether the output is parsed and returned.

returnThinking.title: Return Thinking
returnThinking.description:
Controls whether to return the model's internal reasoning or 'thinking' text, if available. When enabled,
the reasoning content is extracted from the response and made available in the AiMessage object.
It Does not trigger the thinking process itself—only affects whether the output is parsed and returned.

anyOf.title: Enable Thinking
anyOf.description:
Enables internal reasoning ('thinking') in supported language models, allowing the model to perform intermediate reasoning steps
before producing a final output; this is useful for complex tasks like multi-step problem solving or decision making, but may
increase token usage and response time, and is only applicable to compatible models.

anyOf.title: Enable Thinking
anyOf.description:
Enables internal reasoning ('thinking') in supported language models, allowing the model to perform intermediate reasoning steps
before producing a final output; this is useful for complex tasks like multi-step problem solving or decision making, but may
increase token usage and response time, and is only applicable to compatible models.

thinkingEnabled.title: Enable Thinking
thinkingEnabled.description:
Enables internal reasoning ('thinking') in supported language models, allowing the model to perform intermediate reasoning steps
before producing a final output; this is useful for complex tasks like multi-step problem solving or decision making, but may
increase token usage and response time, and is only applicable to compatible models.

anyOf.title: Thinking Token Budget
anyOf.description:
Specifies the maximum number of tokens allocated as a budget for internal reasoning processes, such as generating intermediate
thoughts or chain-of-thought sequences, allowing the model to perform multi-step reasoning before producing the final output.

anyOf.title: Thinking Token Budget
anyOf.description:
Specifies the maximum number of tokens allocated as a budget for internal reasoning processes, such as generating intermediate
thoughts or chain-of-thought sequences, allowing the model to perform multi-step reasoning before producing the final output.

thinkingBudgetTokens.title: Thinking Token Budget
thinkingBudgetTokens.description:
Specifies the maximum number of tokens allocated as a budget for internal reasoning processes, such as generating intermediate
thoughts or chain-of-thought sequences, allowing the model to perform multi-step reasoning before producing the final output.

type.title: Response format type
type.description:
Specifies how the LLM should return output.
Allowed values:
- TEXT (default): free-form natural language.
- JSON: structured output validated against a JSON Schema.

jsonSchema.title: JSON Schema (used when type = JSON)
jsonSchema.description:
Provide a JSON Schema describing the expected structure of the response.
In Kestra flows, define the schema in YAML (it is still a JSON Schema object).
Example (YAML):
```yaml
  responseFormat:
    type: JSON
    jsonSchema:
      type: object
      required: ["category", "priority"]
      properties:
        category:
          type: string
          enum: ["ACCOUNT", "BILLING", "TECHNICAL", "GENERAL"]
        priority:
          type: string
          enum: ["LOW", "MEDIUM", "HIGH"]
```
Note: Provider support for strict schema enforcement varies. If unsupported,
guide the model about the expected output structure via the prompt and validate downstream.

jsonSchemaDescription.title: Schema description (optional)
jsonSchemaDescription.description:
Natural-language description of the schema to help the model produce the right fields.
Example: "Classify a customer ticket into category and priority."

Timeout in seconds for each request

--- title: Classification editLink: false description: "Classify text into provided classes" icon:  ---

Classification

```yaml type: "io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.Classification" ``` Classify text into provided classes Uses an LLM to assign the prompt to exactly one category from `classes`. A default system prompt forces a single-label reply; override it if you need different behavior. Output includes token usage and finish reason. ## Examples > Perform sentiment analysis of product reviews ```yaml id: text_categorization namespace: company.ai tasks: - id: categorize type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.Classification prompt: "Categorize the sentiment of: I love this product!" classes: - positive - negative - neutral provider: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}" modelName: gemini-2.5-flash ``` ## Properties ### `classes` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Classification Options** > The list of possible classification categories ### `prompt` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Text prompt** > The input text to classify ### `provider` * **Type:** * [==AmazonBedrock==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.amazonbedrock) * [==Anthropic==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.anthropic) * [==AzureOpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.azureopenai) * [==DashScope==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.dashscope) * [==DeepSeek==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.deepseek) * [==GitHubModels==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.githubmodels) * [==GoogleGemini==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlegemini) * [==GoogleVertexAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlevertexai) * [==HuggingFace==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.huggingface) * [==LocalAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.localai) * [==MistralAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.mistralai) * [==OciGenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ocigenai) * [==Ollama==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ollama) * [==OpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openai) * [==OpenRouter==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openrouter) * [==WatsonxAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.watsonxai) * [==WorkersAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.workersai) * [==ZhiPuAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.zhipuai) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ✔️ **Language Model Provider** ### `configuration` * **Type:** [==ChatConfiguration==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.domain.chatconfiguration) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `{}` **Chat configuration** ### `systemMessage` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `Respond by only one of the following classes by typing just the exact class name: {{ classes }}` **Optional system message** > Instruction message for the model. Defaults to a standard classification instruction using the provided classes. ## Outputs ### `classification` * **Type:** ==string== * **Required:** ❌ **Classification Result** > The classified category of the input text ### `finishReason` * **Type:** ==string== * **Required:** ❌ * **Possible Values:** * `STOP` * `LENGTH` * `TOOL_EXECUTION` * `CONTENT_FILTER` * `OTHER` **Finish reason** ### `tokenUsage` * **Type:** [==TokenUsage==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.domain.tokenusage) * **Required:** ❌ **Token usage** ## Definitions ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.AzureOpenAI` #### Properties ##### `endpoint` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **API endpoint** > The Azure OpenAI endpoint in the format: https://{resource}.openai.azure.com/ ##### `modelName` * **Type:** Example (YAML): ```yaml responseFormat: type: JSON jsonSchema: type: object required: ["category", "priority"] properties: category: type: string enum: ["ACCOUNT", "BILLING", "TECHNICAL", "GENERAL"] priority: type: string enum: ["LOW", "MEDIUM", "HIGH"] ``` Note: Provider support for strict schema enforcement varies. If unsupported, guide the model about the expected output structure via the prompt and validate downstream. ##### `jsonSchemaDescription` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Schema description (optional)** > Natural-language description of the schema to help the model produce the right fields. ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Log LLM responses** > If true, raw responses from the LLM will be logged at INFO level. ##### `maxToken` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ > Maximum number of tokens the model can generate in the completion (response). This limits the length of the output. ##### `responseFormat` * **Type:** [==ChatConfiguration-ResponseFormat==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.domain.chatconfiguration-responseformat) * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ **Response format** > Defines the expected output format. Default is plain text. Some providers allow requesting JSON or schema-constrained outputs, but support varies and may be incompatible with tool use. When using a JSON schema, the output will be returned under the key `jsonOutput`. ##### `returnThinking` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Return Thinking** > Controls whether to return the model's internal reasoning or 'thinking' text, if available. When enabled, the reasoning content is extracted from the response and made available in the AiMessage object. It Does not trigger the thinking process itself—only affects whether the output is parsed and returned. ##### `seed` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Seed** > Optional random seed for reproducibility. Provide a positive integer (e.g., 42, 1234). Using the same seed with identical settings produces repeatable outputs. ##### `temperature` * **Type:** * ==number== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Temperature** > Controls randomness in generation. Typical range is 0.0–1.0. Lower values (e.g., 0.2) make outputs more focused and deterministic, while higher values (e.g., 0.7–1.0) increase creativity and variability. ##### `thinkingBudgetTokens` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Thinking Token Budget** > Specifies the maximum number of tokens allocated as a budget for internal reasoning processes, such as generating intermediate thoughts or chain-of-thought sequences, allowing the model to perform multi-step reasoning before producing the final output. ##### `thinkingEnabled` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Enable Thinking** > Enables internal reasoning ('thinking') in supported language models, allowing the model to perform intermediate reasoning steps before producing a final output; this is useful for complex tasks like multi-step problem solving or decision making, but may increase token usage and response time, and is only applicable to compatible models. ##### `topK` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Top-K** > Limits sampling to the top K most likely tokens at each step. Typical values are between 20 and 100. Smaller values reduce randomness; larger values allow more diverse outputs. ##### `topP` * **Type:** * ==number== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Top-P (nucleus sampling)** > Selects from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability is ≤ topP. Typical values are 0.8–0.95. Lower values make the output more focused, higher values increase diversity. ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.DashScope` #### Properties ##### `apiKey` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **API Key** ##### `modelName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Model name** ##### `type` * **Type:** ==object== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ✔️ ##### `baseUrl` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/api/v1` **API base URL** > If you use a model in the China (Beijing) region, you need to replace the URL with: https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/api/v1, otherwise use the Singapore region of: "https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/api/v1. The default value is computed based on the system timezone. ##### `caPem` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **CA PEM certificate content** > CA certificate as text, used to verify SSL/TLS connections when using custom endpoints. ##### `clientPem` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Client PEM certificate content** > PEM client certificate as text, used to authenticate the connection to enterprise AI endpoints. ##### `enableSearch` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Whether the model uses Internet search results for reference when generating text or not** ##### `maxTokens` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **The maximum number of tokens returned by this request** ##### `repetitionPenalty` * **Type:** * ==number== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Repetition in a continuous sequence during model generation** > Increasing repetition_penalty reduces the repetition in model generation, 1.0 means no penalty. task_description: Calls the configured image model to create an image from the provided text prompt and returns the image URL. Output includes finish reason and token usage; image size/format limits follow the provider model.

$examples.code:
id: image_generation
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: image_generation
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ImageGeneration
    prompt: >
      Four-panel comic page about a data engineer shipping a workflow.
      Clean modern line art with soft colors and ample white space.
      Panel 1: Early morning desk setup with dual monitors, coffee, and a workflow DAG on screen; calm focused mood.
      Panel 2: Debugging a failing task; close-up of terminal and error icon; speech bubble: "hmm…"
      Panel 3: Fix applied; green checks ripple through the pipeline; small celebratory detail (cat paw, fist pump).
      Panel 4: Deployed dashboard showing metrics trending up; sticky note says "ship it".
      Include subtle tech props (cloud icons, database cylinder) but no logos.
      Minimal readable text only in tiny bubbles/notes; no large paragraphs of text.
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.OpenAI
      apiKey: "{{ kv('OPENAI_API_KEY') }}"
      modelName: dall-e-3
$examples.title: Generate an image using OpenAI (DALL-E 3)

prompt.title: Image prompt
prompt.description: The input prompt for the image generation model

properties.description: Calls the configured image model to create an image from the provided text prompt and returns the image URL. Output includes finish reason and token usage; image size/format limits follow the provider model.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.Ollama.title: Use local Ollama models

$examples.code:
id: chat_completion
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING

tasks:
  - id: chat_completion
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.Ollama
      modelName: llama3
      endpoint: http://localhost:11434
      thinkingEnabled: true
      returnThinking: true
    messages:
      - type: SYSTEM
        content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity.
      - type: USER
        content: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
$examples.title: Chat completion with Ollama

task_markdown:
--- title: ImageGeneration editLink: false description: "Generate images from a prompt" icon:  ---

ImageGeneration

```yaml type: "io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ImageGeneration" ``` Generate images from a prompt Calls the configured image model to create an image from the provided text prompt and returns the image URL. Output includes finish reason and token usage; image size/format limits follow the provider model. ## Examples > Generate an image using OpenAI (DALL-E 3) ```yaml id: image_generation namespace: company.ai tasks: - id: image_generation type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ImageGeneration prompt: > Four-panel comic page about a data engineer shipping a workflow. Clean modern line art with soft colors and ample white space. Panel 1: Early morning desk setup with dual monitors, coffee, and a workflow DAG on screen; calm focused mood. Panel 2: Debugging a failing task; close-up of terminal and error icon; speech bubble: "hmm…" Panel 3: Fix applied; green checks ripple through the pipeline; small celebratory detail (cat paw, fist pump). Panel 4: Deployed dashboard showing metrics trending up; sticky note says "ship it". Include subtle tech props (cloud icons, database cylinder) but no logos. Minimal readable text only in tiny bubbles/notes; no large paragraphs of text. provider: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.OpenAI apiKey: "{{ kv('OPENAI_API_KEY') }}" modelName: dall-e-3 ``` ## Properties ### `prompt` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Image prompt** > The input prompt for the image generation model ### `provider` * **Type:** * [==AmazonBedrock==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.amazonbedrock) * [==Anthropic==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.anthropic) * [==AzureOpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.azureopenai) * [==DashScope==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.dashscope) * [==DeepSeek==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.deepseek) * [==GitHubModels==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.githubmodels) * [==GoogleGemini==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlegemini) * [==GoogleVertexAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlevertexai) * [==HuggingFace==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.huggingface) * [==LocalAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.localai) * [==MistralAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.mistralai) * [==OciGenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ocigenai) * [==Ollama==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ollama) * [==OpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openai) * [==OpenRouter==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openrouter) * [==WatsonxAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.watsonxai) * [==WorkersAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.workersai) * [==ZhiPuAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.zhipuai) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ✔️ **Language Model Provider** ## Outputs ### `finishReason` * **Type:** ==string== * **Required:** ❌ * **Possible Values:** * `STOP` * `LENGTH` * `TOOL_EXECUTION` * `CONTENT_FILTER` * `OTHER` **Finish reason** ### `imageUrl` * **Type:** ==string== * **Required:** ❌ **Generated image URL** > The URL of the generated image ### `tokenUsage` * **Type:** [==TokenUsage==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.domain.tokenusage) * **Required:** ❌ **Token usage** ## Definitions ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.AzureOpenAI` #### Properties ##### `endpoint` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **API endpoint** > The Azure OpenAI endpoint in the format: https://{resource}.openai.azure.com/ ##### `modelName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Model name** ##### `type` * **Type:** ==object== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ✔️ * **Possible Values:** * `io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.AzureOpenAI` * `io.kestra.plugin.langchain4j.provider.AzureOpenAI` ##### `apiKey` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **API Key** ##### `baseUrl` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Base URL** > Custom base URL to override the default endpoint (useful for local tests, WireMock, or enterprise gateways). ##### `caPem` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **CA PEM certificate content** > CA certificate as text, used to verify SSL/TLS connections when using custom endpoints. ##### `clientId` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Client ID** ##### `clientPem` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Client PEM certificate content** > PEM client certificate as text, used to authenticate the connection to enterprise AI endpoints. ##### `clientSecret` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Client secret** ##### `serviceVersion` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **API version** ##### `tenantId` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Tenant ID** ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleVertexAI` #### Properties ##### `endpoint` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Endpoint URL** ##### `location` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Project location** ##### `modelName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Model name** ##### `project` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Project ID** ##### `type` * task_description: Builds a JSON schema from `jsonFields` (all required) and asks the model to return compliant JSON for the given prompt. Requires a provider that supports JSON response formats; otherwise include schema hints in the prompt. Returns extracted JSON, token usage, and finish reason.

schemaName.title: Schema Name
schemaName.description: The schema name used for the structured JSON extraction

properties.title: Extract JSON fields from text

$metrics.name: input.token.count
$metrics.description: Large Language Model (LLM) input token count

$metrics.name: output.token.count
$metrics.description: Large Language Model (LLM) output token count

$metrics.name: total.token.count
$metrics.description: Large Language Model (LLM) total token count

$examples.code:
id: json_structured_extraction
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: extract_person
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.JSONStructuredExtraction
    schemaName: Person
    jsonFields:
      - name
      - city
      - country
      - email
    prompt: |
      From the text below, extract the person's name, city, and email.
      If a field is missing, leave it blank.

      Text:
      "Hi! I'm John Smith from Paris, France. You can reach me at john.smith@example.com."
    systemMessage: You extract structured data in JSON format.
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
      modelName: gemini-2.5-flash
$examples.title: Extract person fields (Gemini)

$examples.code:
id: json_structured_extraction_order
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: extract_order
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.JSONStructuredExtraction
    schemaName: Order
    jsonFields:
      - order_id
      - customer_name
      - city
      - total_amount
    prompt: |
      Extract the order_id, customer_name, city, and total_amount from the message.
      For the total amount, keep only the number without the currency symbol.
      Return only JSON with the requested keys.

      Message:
      "Order #A-1043 for Jane Doe, shipped to Berlin. Total: 249.99 EUR."
    systemMessage: You are a precise JSON data extraction assistant.
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.OpenAI
      apiKey: "{{ kv('OPENAI_API_KEY') }}"
      modelName: gpt-5-mini
$examples.title: Extract order details (OpenAI)

prompt.title: Text prompt
prompt.description: The input text for structured JSON extraction.

jsonFields.title: JSON Fields
jsonFields.description: List of fields to extract from the text

schemaName.title: Schema Name
schemaName.description: The name of the JSON schema for structured extraction

systemMessage.title: System message
systemMessage.default: You are a structured JSON extraction assistant. Always respond with valid JSON.
systemMessage.description: Optional system instruction for the model.

properties.description: Builds a JSON schema from `jsonFields` (all required) and asks the model to return compliant JSON for the given prompt. Requires a provider that supports JSON response formats; otherwise include schema hints in the prompt. Returns extracted JSON, token usage, and finish reason.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.Ollama.title: Use local Ollama models

$examples.code:
id: chat_completion
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING

tasks:
  - id: chat_completion
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.Ollama
      modelName: llama3
      endpoint: http://localhost:11434
      thinkingEnabled: true
      returnThinking: true
    messages:
      - type: SYSTEM
        content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity.
      - type: USER
        content: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
$examples.title: Chat completion with Ollama

seed.description: Optional random seed for reproducibility. Provide a positive integer (e.g., 42, 1234). Using the same seed with identical settings produces repeatable outputs.

anyOf.title: Top-K
anyOf.description: Limits sampling to the top K most likely tokens at each step. Typical values are between 20 and 100. Smaller values reduce randomness; larger values allow more diverse outputs.

anyOf.title: Top-P (nucleus sampling)
anyOf.description: Selects from the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability is ≤ topP. Typical values are 0.8–0.95. Lower values make the output more focused, higher values increase diversity.
