task_markdown:
--- title: IngestDocument editLink: false description: "Ingest documents into embeddings" icon:  ---

IngestDocument

```yaml type: "io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument" ``` Ingest documents into embeddings Loads text from local path, internal storage, URLs, or inline docs; splits with the chosen splitter; then writes embeddings to the configured store. `drop=true` clears the store first. Default splitter is absent; provide one for chunking. ## Examples > Ingest documents into a KV embedding store. WARNING: the KV embedding store is for quick prototyping only; it stores embedding vectors in a KV store and loads them all into memory. ```yaml id: document_ingestion namespace: company.ai tasks: - id: ingest type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument provider: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07 apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}" embeddings: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVStore drop: true fromExternalURLs: - https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kestra-io/docs/refs/heads/main/content/blogs/release-0-24.md ``` ## Properties ### `embeddings` * **Type:** * [==Chroma==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.chroma) * [==Elasticsearch==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.elasticsearch) * [==KestraKVStore==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.kestrakvstore) * [==MariaDB==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.mariadb) * [==Milvus==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.milvus) * [==MongoDBAtlas==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.mongodbatlas) * [==PGVector==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.pgvector) * [==Pinecone==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.pinecone) * [==Qdrant==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.qdrant) * [==Redis==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.redis) * [==Tablestore==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.tablestore) * [==Weaviate==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.weaviate) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ✔️ **Embedding store provider** ### `provider` * **Type:** * [==AmazonBedrock==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.amazonbedrock) * [==Anthropic==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.anthropic) * [==AzureOpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.azureopenai) * [==DashScope==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.dashscope) * [==DeepSeek==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.deepseek) * [==GitHubModels==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.githubmodels) * [==GoogleGemini==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlegemini) * [==GoogleVertexAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlevertexai) * [==HuggingFace==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.huggingface) * [==LocalAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.localai) * [==MistralAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.mistralai) * [==OciGenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ocigenai) * [==Ollama==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ollama) * [==OpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openai) * [==OpenRouter==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openrouter) * [==WatsonxAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.watsonxai) * [==WorkersAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.workersai) * [==ZhiPuAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.zhipuai) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ✔️ **Language model provider** > Must be configured with an embedding model. ### `bulkSize` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `500` **Bulk ingestion size** > Maximum number of documents sent per ingestion request. ### `documentSplitter` * **Type:** [==IngestDocument-DocumentSplitter==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.ingestdocument-documentsplitter) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ❌ **Document splitter** ### `drop` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `false` **Drop the store before ingestion (useful for testing)** ### `fromDocuments` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** [==IngestDocument-InlineDocument==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.ingestdocument-inlinedocument) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ❌ **List of inline documents** ### `fromExternalURLs` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **List of document URLs from external sources** ### `fromInternalURIs` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **List of internal storage URIs for documents** ### `fromPath` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Path in the task working directory containing documents to ingest** > Each document in the directory will be ingested into the embedding store. Ingestion is recursive and protected against path traversal (CWE-22). ### `metadata` * **Type:** ==object== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Additional metadata to add to all ingested documents** ## Outputs ### `embeddingStoreOutputs` * **Type:** ==object== * **Required:** ❌ **Additional outputs from the embedding store** ### `ingestedDocuments` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Required:** ❌ **Number of ingested documents** ### `inputTokenCount` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Required:** ❌ **Input token count** ### `outputTokenCount` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Required:** ❌ **Output token count** ### `totalTokenCount` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Required:** ❌ **Total token count** ## Definitions ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.PGVector` #### Properties ##### `database` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database name** ##### `host` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database server host** ##### `password` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database password** ##### `port` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database server port** ##### `table` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The table to store embeddings in** ##### `type` * **Type:** ==object== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ✔️ * **Possible Values:** * `io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.PGVector` * `io.kestra.plugin.langchain4j.embeddings.PGVector` ##### `user` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database user** ##### `useIndex` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `false` **Whether to use use an IVFFlat index** > An IVFFlat index divides vectors into lists, and then searches a subset of those lists closest to the query vector. It has faster build times and uses less memory than HNSW but has lower query performance (in terms of speed-recall tradeoff). ### `com.alicloud.openservices.tablestore.model.search.FieldSchema` #### Properties ##### `analyzer` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ * **Possible Values:** * `SingleWord` * `MaxWord` * `MinWord` * `Split` * `Fuzzy` ##### `analyzerParameter` * **Type:** [==AnalyzerParameter==](#com.alicloud.openservices.tablestore.model.search.analysis.analyzerparameter) * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `dateFormats` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `enableHighlighting` * **Type:** ==boolean== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `enableSortAndAgg` * **Type:** ==boolean== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `fieldName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `fieldType` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ * **Possible Values:** * `LONG` * `DOUBLE` * `BOOLEAN` * `KEYWORD` * `TEXT` * `NESTED` * `GEO_POINT` * `DATE` * `VECTOR` * `FUZZY_KEYWORD` * `IP` * `JSON` * `UNKNOWN` ##### `index` * **Type:** ==boolean== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `indexOptions` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ * **Possible Values:** * `DOCS` * `FREQS` * `POSITIONS` * `OFFSETS` ##### `isArray` * **Type:** ==boolean== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `jsonType` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ * **Possible Values:** * `FLATTEN` * `NESTED` ##### `sourceFieldNames` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `store` * **Type:** ==boolean== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❌ ##### `subFieldSchemas` * Depends on Milvus deployment and dataset. ##### `metadataFieldName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Metadata field name** > Field name for metadata. Default depends on collection schema. ##### `metricType` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Metric type** > Similarity metric (e.g., L2, IP, COSINE). Should match the embedding provider’s expected metric. ##### `password` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Password** > Required when authentication/TLS is enabled. See https://milvus.io/docs/authenticate.md ##### `port` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Port** > Milvus port (used when `uri` is not set). Typical: 19530 (gRPC) or 9091 (HTTP). Default: 19530. ##### `retrieveEmbeddingsOnSearch` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Retrieve embeddings on search** > If true, return stored embeddings along with matches. Default depends on collection schema. ##### `uri` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **URI** > Connection URI. Use either `uri` OR `host`/`port` (not both). Examples: - gRPC (typical): "milvus://host:19530" - HTTP: "http://host:9091" ##### `username` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Username** > Required when authentication/TLS is enabled. task_title: Search embeddings and optionally return results
task_description: Runs semantic search against the configured embedding store using the provided query embedding. `maxResults` limits hits and `minScore` filters low-similarity matches. `fetchType` controls output: NONE (metrics only), FETCH/FETCH_ONE (return matches), or STORE (write matches to internal storage).

uri.title: Output file URI in Kestra’s internal storage
uri.description: Only available when `fetchType` is set to `STORE`

size.title: Count of fetched or stored items

properties.title: Search embeddings and optionally return results

$metrics.name: fetch.size
$metrics.description: The number of rows fetch from the embedding store.

$examples.code:
id: search_embeddings_flow
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: ingest
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    embeddings:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVStore
    drop: true
    fromExternalURLs:
      - https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kestra-io/docs/refs/heads/main/content/blogs/release-0-22.md

  - id: search
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.Search
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    embeddings:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVStore
    query: "Feature Highlights"
    maxResults: 5
    minScore: 0.5
    fetchType: FETCH
$examples.title: Search an embedding store

properties.description: Runs semantic search against the configured embedding store using the provided query embedding. `maxResults` limits hits and `minScore` filters low-similarity matches. `fetchType` controls output: NONE (metrics only), FETCH/FETCH_ONE (return matches), or STORE (write matches to internal storage).

io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.Ollama.title: Use local Ollama models

$examples.code:
id: chat_completion
namespace: company.ai

inputs:
  - id: prompt
    type: STRING

tasks:
  - id: chat_completion
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.completion.ChatCompletion
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.Ollama
      modelName: llama3
      endpoint: http://localhost:11434
      thinkingEnabled: true
      returnThinking: true
    messages:
      - type: SYSTEM
        content: You are a helpful assistant, answer concisely, avoid overly casual language or unnecessary verbosity.
      - type: USER
        content: "{{inputs.prompt}}"
$examples.title: Chat completion with Ollama

io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.Ollama.description: Calls an Ollama server for chat/embeddings using the given endpoint and model name. Ideal for self-hosted/local models; ensure the Ollama daemon is reachable.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Redis.title: Store embeddings in Redis

$examples.code:
id: document_ingestion
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: ingest
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Redis.description: Backs an embedding index with Redis (jedis). Uses `indexName` (defaults to `embedding-index`); `drop=true` clears stored vectors. Ensure Redis deployment supports vector search modules for production use.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Milvus.title: Store embeddings in Milvus

$examples.code:
id: document_ingestion
namespace: company.ai

tasks:
  - id: ingest
    type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument
    provider:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini
      modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07
      apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}"
    embeddings:
      type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Milvus
      # Use either `uri` or `host`/`port`:
      # For gRPC (typical): milvus://localhost:19530
      # For HTTP: http://localhost:9091
      uri: "http://localhost:19200"
      token: "{{ kv('MILVUS_TOKEN') }}"  # omit if auth is disabled
      collectionName: embeddings
    fromExternalURLs:
      - https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kestra-io/docs/refs/heads/main/content/blogs/release-0-24.md
$examples.title: Ingest documents into a Milvus embedding store

uri.title: URI
uri.description:
Connection URI. Use either `uri` OR `host`/`port` (not both).
Examples:
- gRPC (typical): "milvus://host:19530"
- HTTP: "http://host:9091"

host.title: Host
host.description: Milvus host name (used when `uri` is not set). Default: "localhost".

anyOf.title: Port
anyOf.description: Milvus port (used when `uri` is not set). Typical: 19530 (gRPC) or 9091 (HTTP). Default: 19530.

anyOf.title: Port
anyOf.description: Milvus port (used when `uri` is not set). Typical: 19530 (gRPC) or 9091 (HTTP). Default: 19530.

port.title: Port
port.description: Milvus port (used when `uri` is not set). Typical: 19530 (gRPC) or 9091 (HTTP). Default: 19530.

token.title: Token
token.description: Milvus auth token. Required if authentication is enabled; omit for local deployments without auth.

password.title: Password
password.description: Required when authentication/TLS is enabled. See https://milvus.io/docs/authenticate.md

username.title: Username
username.description: Required when authentication/TLS is enabled. See https://milvus.io/docs/authenticate.md

indexType.title: Index type

vectorFieldName.title: Vector field name

consistencyLevel.title: Consistency level

indexes.title: Metadata Index Definitions

List of SQL index definitions for metadata columns (e.g., 'INDEX idx_text (text)').
 Used only with COLUMN_PER_KEY storage mode.

anyOf.title: Whether to create the table if it doesn't exist
Whether to create the table if it doesn't exist

createTable.title: Whether to create the table if it doesn't exist

columnDefinitions.title: Metadata Column Definitions
columnDefinitions.description:
List of SQL column definitions for metadata fields (e.g., 'text TEXT', 'source TEXT').
  Required only when using COLUMN_PER_KEY storage mode.

metadataStorageMode.title: Metadata Storage Mode
metadataStorageMode.description:
Determines how metadata is stored:
    - COLUMN_PER_KEY: Use individual columns for each metadata field (requires columnDefinitions and indexes).
    - COMBINED_JSON (default): Store metadata as a JSON object in a single column.
  If columnDefinitions and indexes are provided, COLUMN_PER_KEY must be used.

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.MariaDB.description: Persists embeddings to a MariaDB table; create/drop behavior is controlled by `createTable` and `drop`. Metadata defaults to COMBINED_JSON unless COLUMN_PER_KEY is configured with column/index definitions. Requires valid JDBC URL and credentials.

table.title: The table to store embeddings in

anyOf.title: Whether to use use an IVFFlat index
anyOf.description: An IVFFlat index divides vectors into lists, and then searches a subset of those lists closest to the query vector. It has faster build times and uses less memory than HNSW but has lower query performance (in terms of speed-recall tradeoff).

anyOf.title: Whether to use use an IVFFlat index
anyOf.description: An IVFFlat index divides vectors into lists, and then searches a subset of those lists closest to the query vector. It has faster build times and uses less memory than HNSW but has lower query performance (in terms of speed-recall tradeoff).

useIndex.title: Whether to use use an IVFFlat index
useIndex.description: An IVFFlat index divides vectors into lists, and then searches a subset of those lists closest to the query vector. It has faster build times and uses less memory than HNSW but has lower query performance (in terms of speed-recall tradeoff).

io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.PGVector.description: Uses the PostgreSQL pgvector extension to persist embeddings in the given table. `drop=true` recreates the table; optional IVF index (`useIndex`) defaults to false. Ensure pgvector extension is installed and the user can create tables/indexes.

task_markdown:
--- title: Search editLink: false description: "Search embeddings and optionally return results" icon:  ---

Search

```yaml type: "io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.Search" ``` Search embeddings and optionally return results Runs semantic search against the configured embedding store using the provided query embedding. `maxResults` limits hits and `minScore` filters low-similarity matches. `fetchType` controls output: NONE (metrics only), FETCH/FETCH_ONE (return matches), or STORE (write matches to internal storage). ## Examples > Search an embedding store ```yaml id: search_embeddings_flow namespace: company.ai tasks: - id: ingest type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.IngestDocument provider: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07 apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}" embeddings: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVStore drop: true fromExternalURLs: - https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kestra-io/docs/refs/heads/main/content/blogs/release-0-22.md - id: search type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.rag.Search provider: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.GoogleGemini modelName: gemini-embedding-exp-03-07 apiKey: "{{ kv('GEMINI_API_KEY') }}" embeddings: type: io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.KestraKVStore query: "Feature Highlights" maxResults: 5 minScore: 0.5 fetchType: FETCH ``` ## Properties ### `embeddings` * **Type:** * [==Chroma==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.chroma) * [==Elasticsearch==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.elasticsearch) * [==KestraKVStore==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.kestrakvstore) * [==MariaDB==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.mariadb) * [==Milvus==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.milvus) * [==MongoDBAtlas==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.mongodbatlas) * [==PGVector==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.pgvector) * [==Pinecone==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.pinecone) * [==Qdrant==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.qdrant) * [==Redis==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.redis) * [==Tablestore==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.tablestore) * [==Weaviate==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.weaviate) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ✔️ **Embedding store provider** ### `maxResults` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Maximum number of results** ### `minScore` * **Type:** * ==number== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Minimum similarity score** ### `provider` * **Type:** * [==AmazonBedrock==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.amazonbedrock) * [==Anthropic==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.anthropic) * [==AzureOpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.azureopenai) * [==DashScope==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.dashscope) * [==DeepSeek==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.deepseek) * [==GitHubModels==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.githubmodels) * [==GoogleGemini==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlegemini) * [==GoogleVertexAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.googlevertexai) * [==HuggingFace==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.huggingface) * [==LocalAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.localai) * [==MistralAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.mistralai) * [==OciGenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ocigenai) * [==Ollama==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.ollama) * [==OpenAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openai) * [==OpenRouter==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.openrouter) * [==WatsonxAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.watsonxai) * [==WorkersAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.workersai) * [==ZhiPuAI==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.zhipuai) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ✔️ **Embedding model provider** ### `query` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Query string** ### `fetchType` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `NONE` * **Possible Values:** * `STORE` * `FETCH` * `FETCH_ONE` * `NONE` ## Outputs ### `results` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Required:** ❌ **List of matching text results** ### `size` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Required:** ❌ **Count of fetched or stored items** ### `uri` * **Type:** ==string== * **Required:** ❌ * **Format:** `uri` **Output file URI in Kestra’s internal storage** > Only available when `fetchType` is set to `STORE` ## Definitions ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.PGVector` #### Properties ##### `database` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database name** ##### `host` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database server host** ##### `password` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database password** ##### `port` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database server port** ##### `table` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The table to store embeddings in** ##### `type` * **Type:** ==object== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ✔️ * **Possible Values:** * `io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.PGVector` * `io.kestra.plugin.langchain4j.embeddings.PGVector` ##### `user` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The database user** ##### `useIndex` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `false` **Whether to use use an IVFFlat index** > An IVFFlat index divides vectors into lists, and then searches a subset of those lists closest to the query vector. It has faster build times and uses less memory than HNSW but has lower query performance (in terms of speed-recall tradeoff). ### `com.alicloud.openservices.tablestore.model.search.FieldSchema` #### Depends on Milvus deployment and dataset. ##### `metadataFieldName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Metadata field name** > Field name for metadata. Default depends on collection schema. ##### `metricType` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Metric type** > Similarity metric (e.g., L2, IP, COSINE). Should match the embedding provider’s expected metric. ##### `password` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Password** > Required when authentication/TLS is enabled. See https://milvus.io/docs/authenticate.md ##### `port` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Port** > Milvus port (used when `uri` is not set). Typical: 19530 (gRPC) or 9091 (HTTP). Default: 19530. ##### `retrieveEmbeddingsOnSearch` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Retrieve embeddings on search** > If true, return stored embeddings along with matches. Default: false. ##### `textFieldName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Text field name** > Field name for original text. Default depends on collection schema. ##### `uri` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **URI** > Connection URI. Use either `uri` OR `host`/`port` (not both). Examples: - gRPC (typical): "milvus://host:19530" - HTTP: "http://host:9091" ##### `username` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Username** > Required when authentication/TLS is enabled. See https://milvus.io/docs/authenticate.md ##### `vectorFieldName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Vector field name** > Field name for the embedding vector. HTTP remains required for search operations. ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.Elasticsearch-ElasticsearchConnection` #### Properties ##### `hosts` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ * **Min items:** `1` **List of HTTP Elasticsearch servers** > Must be a URI like `https://example.com:9200` with scheme and port ##### `basicAuth` * **Type:** [==Elasticsearch-ElasticsearchConnection-BasicAuth==](#io.kestra.plugin.ai.embeddings.elasticsearch-elasticsearchconnection-basicauth) * **Dynamic:** ❌ * **Required:** ❌ **Basic authorization configuration** ##### `headers` * **Type:** ==array== * **SubType:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **List of HTTP headers to be sent with every request** > Each item is a `key: value` string, e.g., `Authorization: Token XYZ` ##### `pathPrefix` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Path prefix for all HTTP requests** > If set to `/my/path`, each client request becomes `/my/path/` + endpoint. Useful when Elasticsearch is behind a proxy providing a base path; do not use otherwise. ##### `strictDeprecationMode` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Treat responses with deprecation warnings as failures** ##### `trustAllSsl` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Trust all SSL CA certificates** > Use this if the server uses a self-signed SSL certificate ### `io.kestra.plugin.ai.provider.DashScope` #### Properties ##### `apiKey` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **API Key** ##### `modelName` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **Model name** ##### `type` * **Type:** ==object== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ✔️ ##### `baseUrl` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/api/v1` **API base URL** > If you use a model in the China (Beijing) region, you need to replace the URL with: https://dashscope.aliyuncs.com/api/v1, otherwise use the Singapore region of: "https://dashscope-intl.aliyuncs.com/api/v1. The default value is computed based on the system timezone. ##### `caPem` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **CA PEM certificate content** > CA certificate as text, used to verify SSL/TLS connections when using custom endpoints. ##### `clientPem` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Client PEM certificate content** > PEM client certificate as text, used to authenticate the connection to enterprise AI endpoints. ##### `enableSearch` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Whether the model uses Internet search results for reference when generating text or not** ##### `maxTokens` * **Type:** * ==integer== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **The maximum number of tokens returned by this request** ##### `repetitionPenalty` * **Type:** * ==number== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Repetition in a continuous sequence during model generation** > Increasing repetition_penalty reduces the repetition in model generation, 1.0 means no penalty. task_markdown:
--- title: Reset editLink: false description: "Reset an Airbyte Cloud job." icon:  ---

Reset

```yaml type: "io.kestra.plugin.airbyte.cloud.jobs.Reset" ``` Reset an Airbyte Cloud job. ## Examples ```yaml id: airbyte_reset namespace: company.team tasks: - id: reset type: io.kestra.plugin.airbyte.cloud.jobs.Reset token: "{{ secret('AIRBYTE_TOKEN') }}" connectionId: e3b1ce92-547c-436f-b1e8-23b6936c12cd ``` ## Properties ### `connectionId` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ✔️ **The connection ID to sync** ### `clientId` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Application Client ID.** ### `clientSecret` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **Application Client Secret.** ### `maxDuration` * **Type:** * ==string== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `PT1H` **The maximum total wait duration** ### `password` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **BasicAuth authentication password.** ### `pollFrequency` * **Type:** * ==string== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `PT1S` **Specifies the frequency for state check API call** ### `token` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **API key.** ### `tokenURL` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `https://api.airbyte.com/v1/applications/token` **Token URL to get an access token from.** > See: https://reference.airbyte.com/reference/createaccesstoken ### `username` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ **BasicAuth authentication username.** ### `wait` * **Type:** * ==boolean== * ==string== * **Dynamic:** ✔️ * **Required:** ❌ * **Default:** `true` **Wait for the job to end** > Allows the capture of job status & logs ## Outputs ### `job` * **Type:** [==AbstractTrigger-Job==](#io.kestra.plugin.airbyte.cloud.jobs.abstracttrigger-job) * **Required:** ❌ **The job created** ## Definitions ### `io.kestra.plugin.airbyte.cloud.jobs.AbstractTrigger-Job` #### Properties ##### `bytesSynced` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❓ ##### `duration` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❓ * **Format:** `duration` ##### `jobId` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❓ ##### `jobType` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❓ * **Possible Values:** * `SYNC` * `RESET` * `REFRESH` * `CLEAR` ##### `lastUpdatedAt` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❓ * **Format:** `date-time` ##### `rowsSynced` * **Type:** ==integer== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❓ ##### `startTime` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❓ * **Format:** `date-time` ##### `status` * **Type:** ==string== * **Dynamic:** ❓ * **Required:** ❓ * **Possible Values:** * `PENDING` * `RUNNING` * `INCOMPLETE` * `FAILED` * `SUCCEEDED` * `CANCELLED` ## Metrics ### `bytes_synced` * **Type:** ==counter== ### `rows_synced` * **Type:** ==counter== ### `duration` * **Type:** ==timer==
